description_long: This object is a handheld digital voice recorder, specifically an Olympus model, featuring a rectangular body with a large display screen occupying the upper half of the front face. Below the screen, there is a cluster of physical buttons, including a central circular navigation pad with directional arrows and an 'OK' button, as well as additional buttons for menu navigation and recording functions. The device is primarily black with subtle gray accents, and it has a compact, elongated form factor designed for portability. The sides and back are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing its streamlined, utilitarian design. The front face is the most detailed, with visible branding, button labels, and indicator lights. This type of device is commonly used for audio recording in urban environments, such as interviews, meetings, or field notes.
description: A compact, black Olympus digital voice recorder with a large display and multiple physical control buttons on the front. Designed for handheld use, it features a sleek, rectangular form and clear branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the display screen, Olympus branding, and all control buttons including the navigation pad and recording controls.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile of the device, with no visible buttons or features.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, featureless back surface, emphasizing the device's rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile with no visible controls or ports.
